title: Python Functions
summary: Defining and calling functions, passing arguments, returning values, and understanding why functions are the basic unit of reusable code.
common_mistakes: Forgetting that a function without return returns None
practice_idea: Write a function that takes a list of grades and returns the average, then a second function that reuses it to grade several students.

title: Python Scope and Namespaces
summary: Where names live and how Python resolves them, following the LEGB rule: Local, Enclosing, Global, Built-in.
common_mistakes: Assigning to a global variable inside a function without declaring it global
Shadowing a built-in name like list or sum
Expecting a loop variable to be scoped to the loop
practice_idea: Write a counter function that fails because of scope, observe the UnboundLocalError, then fix it with nonlocal.

title: Python Closures
summary: Functions defined inside functions that capture variables from their enclosing scope, and keep them alive after the outer function returns.
common_mistakes: Believing the captured variable is copied instead of referenced
Trying to rebind a captured variable without nonlocal
Confusing a closure with a class instance
practice_idea: Write a make_multiplier(n) factory that returns a function multiplying its input by n, and check that two multipliers keep separate values of n.

title: Shell Redirections and Streams
summary: Every process has three standard streams (stdin 0, stdout 1, stderr 2); redirections let you plug them into files or other processes.
common_mistakes: Using > instead of >> and overwriting a file
Checking $? after another command already replaced it
practice_idea: Run a command that produces both output and an error, then separate the two streams into two different files and verify each file's content.
